title: The Kangaroo Courier
location_hint: Outdoor natural area in Australia, kangaroo habitat nearby
theme: A royal KGB mission briefing delivered through the Australian outback, with kangaroos as unwitting couriers

content_brief.tone_notes: KGB spy-agency dossier style — official, mysterious, mission-critical — but kept warm and kid-friendly throughout. Think 'top-secret letter written by a queen who is also a spy handler and very fond of kangaroos.' Difficulty and terrain were not specified by the placer; both defaulted to 2.
must_include: Queen of England, Alina is introduced as the handler/authority figure issuing the mission
Kangaroos appear meaningfully in the story — not just as background colour, but as participants or plot devices in the mission
A dossier-style opening (e.g., 'CLASSIFIED', 'EYES ONLY', 'MISSION BRIEFING') appropriate for young agents
A sense of being trusted with an important royal and spy-agency task
Warm sign-off from Queen Alina acknowledging the finder as a valued KGB operative
must_avoid: Real names of specific public landmarks or exact hiding locations in Australia
Scary, violent, or dark spy content — must stay warm and age-appropriate
Adult-coded spy tropes (weapons, danger, betrayal) — keep stakes lighthearted
